zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin於大約1185年出生在法國時，他的父親Gullaume IV Crespin43歲，母親Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors26歲。他於大約1210年與Amicie de ROYE結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1251年7月28日在他的家鄉享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin于大约1185年出生在法国时，他的父亲Gullaume IV Crespin43岁，母亲Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors26岁。他于大约1210年与Amicie de ROYE结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1251年7月28日在他的家乡享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin was born about 1185, in France, his father, Gullaume IV Crespin, was 43 and his mother, Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors, was 26. He married Amicie de ROYE about 1210. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 28 July 1251, in his hometown, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin est né environ 1185, à France, son père, Gullaume IV Crespin, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Amicie de ROYE environ 1210. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 28 juillet 1251, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin um 1185 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gullaume IV Crespin 43 und seine Mutter Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors 26. Er hat um 1210 Amicie de ROYE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Juli 1251 im Alter von 67 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin è nato il circa 1185 nel Francia, il suo padre, Gullaume IV Crespin, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Amicie de ROYE circa 1210. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 28 luglio 1251, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespinが約1185年にフランスで生まれた時，父Gullaume IV Crespinは43歳，母Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisorsは26歳であった。約1210年にAmicie de ROYEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1251年7月28日に故郷で67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin(이)가 대략 1185년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gullaume IV Crespin(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1210년 Amicie de ROYE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1251년 7월 28일 그의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin nasceu aproximadamente 1185, em França, seu pai, Gullaume IV Crespin, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Amicie de ROYE aproximadamente 1210. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de julho de 1251, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin родился около 1185 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Gullaume IV Crespin было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Amicie de ROYE около 1210 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 28 июля 1251 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume IV du Bec-Crespin nació aproximadamente 1185, en Francia, su papá, Gullaume IV Crespin, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Eve d'Harcourt, Dame de Lisors, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Amicie de ROYE aproximadamente 1210. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 28 de julio de 1251, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años.
